Topic: Decision-making and problem-solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description:
Identify situations when a nutrition-related decision needs to be made (e.g., when trying new foods,
choosing snacks and beverages, eating breakfast). [HPE]

Topic: Decision-making and problem-solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Articulate, with adult guidance, nutrition options in common situations, and identify decisions that can be made independently (e.g., asking for healthier options) or when assistance is needed (e.g., managing a food allergy). [HPE]

Topic: Decision-making and problem-solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Identify that food is the preferred way of obtaining nutrients (as opposed to supplements) and provides energy for the body, identify the primary contribution of each food group to the body, and provide examples of foods within food groups to support informed decision-making. [HPE]

Topic: Decision-making and problem-solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Explain food and health-promoting eating habits in equitable terms (e.g., all people need food access, clean water is necessary, grocery stores are important to communities and their health, access, needs, and preferences vary). [HPE]

Topic: Decision-making and problem-solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Explain benefits of health-promoting eating choices and habits (e.g., balanced eating, staying hydrated, eating fruits and vegetables, eating nutrient-rich foods, eating a variety of foods, limiting foods high in added sugar, awareness of hunger and thirst signals).

Topic: Decision-making and problem-solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Describe how the foods students eat may reflect the area in which they live and/or their cultural backgrounds, ways students? families use or produce food, how family meals and food traditions benefit them, different dietary needs (e.g., food allergies, dietary restrictions) and how they contribute to nutrition-related decisions. [HPE]

Topic: Decision-making and problem-solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Recognize that media (e.g., cartoons, characters, advertisements, product placement) can impact food-related decisions. [HPE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Demonstrate self-control (e.g., delay gratification, wait your turn) alone or with the support of adults in a variety of typical settings (e.g., on the playground, in the classroom, during physical education, at an assembly). [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Define stress and demonstrate strategies for managing stress (e.g., positive self-talk, belly breathing, talking with a trusted adult, listening to calming music, play, physical activity) alone or with the support of adults. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Identify what it means to be responsible and list personal responsibilities. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Utilize simple positive self-talk for the purpose of self-motivation and behavior modification. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Identify simple goals for health, physical activity, academic success, and classroom behavior. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Identify a variety of habits that can promote children?s health (e.g., brushing teeth, proper nutrition, going to the doctor, getting enough sleep, being physically active, limiting screen time, washing hands, etc.). [HPE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Explain how a young person can maintain or enhance the health of both their body and mind. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Use medically accurate names for body parts, including genitals when communicating about their body and physical health. [HPE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Demonstrate independence in health-promoting practices such as hand washing, appropriate teeth brushing motions, sneezing and coughing into elbow, engaging in physical activity, and appropriate use and disposal of tissues. [HPE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Set a simple goal related to physical health habits and monitor progress toward the goal with assistance from an adult. [HPE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Apply strategies for staying safe in a variety of situations (e.g., on the playground, during physical education, around water, when using wheeled recreation, as a pedestrian, around cooking elements or fire, on the bus, when online, around weapons or in situations of gun violence) and determine when to report unsafe situations to an adult.˙[HPE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Provide examples of how rules can keep children safe and identify rules to help children stay safe in various situations (e.g., related to medicines, playground safety, physical education, threats of violence, personal space, and boundaries). [HPE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Demonstrate how to respond (e.g., yell, get away, tell an adult, seek help) and get help in a variety of emergency situations including when and how to call 9-1-1. [HE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Identify safe adults to confide in and places to go if feeling personally threatened (e.g., someone says they will hurt or harm you). [HE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Demonstrate the ability to ask a trusted adult for help (including for problem-solving) in a variety of situations. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Recognize safe, unsafe, and inappropriate touching and demonstrate how to tell a trusted adult if this happens. [HE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Identify and practice behaviors for personal safety: say no, get away, tell a grown-up. [HE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Exhibit positive self-concept, self-esteem, and confidence in abilities in a variety of settings, including practicing new movement skills, demonstrating skills to peers, and participating in cooperative and competitive games and activities. [PE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Recognize body responses, physiological changes in their bodies, and emotions during movement and physical activity. [HPE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Identify and describe strengths and interests related to physical activity and movement skills, and those for which extra effort and practice is needed to experience success. [PE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Exhibit prosocial behavior and contribute to the creation and maintenance of safe, supportive learning environments. [PE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Participate safely in a variety of physical education situations, including independent, partner and group activities, with and without equipment. [PE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Identify physical activity as a health-promoting habit that contributes to overall health and well-being, and list the benefits of these habits on physical well-being (e.g., activities that strengthen the heart and cardiovascular system, contribute to fitness, muscle-building) and mental health (e.g., stress management). [HPE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Identify opportunities, in and out of the school setting, for safe, active play, and physical activity for self-expression, social interaction, personal enjoyment and challenge. [PE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors
Description: Set a short-term physical activity goal relevant to specific needs and abilities, take meaningful action toward achieving the goal, and identify people at home or at school who can help when assistance is needed to achieve the goal.[PE]

Topic: Social Awareness, Relationship, and Communication Skills: Enhance relationships, personal health, and the health of others through social awareness and effective communication.
Description: Define bullying and teasing, explain similarities and differences, and how both can be harmful.

Topic: Social Awareness, Relationship, and Communication Skills: Enhance relationships, personal health, and the health of others through social awareness and effective communication.
Description: Define and demonstrate simple ways to communicate personal boundaries and respect the boundaries of others, including physical, verbal, sexual, and emotional boundaries (e.g., explain why it is important to tell others not to touch their body when they do not want to be touched and why it is important to stop touching someone when they indicate the touch is unwelcome).˙[HPE; SE]

Topic: Social Awareness, Relationship, and Communication Skills: Enhance relationships, personal health, and the health of others through social awareness and effective communication.
Description: Explain how no one has a right to violate personal boundaries, and demonstrate an appropriate refusal (e.g., tell a trusted adult, say NO, leave the situation) when someone says or does something that does not respect personal boundaries. [HPE]

Topic: Social Awareness, Relationship, and Communication Skills: Enhance relationships, personal health, and the health of others through social awareness and effective communication.
Description: Identify groups to which one belongs and reflect on similarities and differences with others. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Social Awareness, Relationship, and Communication Skills: Enhance relationships, personal health, and the health of others through social awareness and effective communication.
Description: Recognize the benefits of and strategies for cooperation in a variety of settings (including physical education). [HPE; SE]

Topic: Social Awareness, Relationship, and Communication Skills: Enhance relationships, personal health, and the health of others through social awareness and effective communication.
Description: Discuss stereotypes, prejudice, discrimination, equality, and inequality and how these can affect relationships and situations. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Social Awareness, Relationship, and Communication Skills: Enhance relationships, personal health, and the health of others through social awareness and effective communication.
Description: Discuss gender-role stereotypes and their potential impacts on people of all genders.˙[HPE]

Topic: Social Awareness, Relationship, and Communication Skills: Enhance relationships, personal health, and the health of others through social awareness and effective communication.
Description: Acknowledge diversity, including (but not limited to) racial, ethnic, religious, dis/ability and cultural differences and traditions, and demonstrate respect for others, and demonstrate empathy and ways to treat all people with dignity and respect. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Social Awareness, Relationship, and Communication Skills: Enhance relationships, personal health, and the health of others through social awareness and effective communication.
Description: Demonstrate awareness of, and ways to show respect for, different family structures (e.g., families with heterosexual parents, families with same-gender parents, single parent families, intergenerational families, adoptive families, foster families). [HE; SE]

Topic: Social Awareness, Relationship, and Communication Skills: Enhance relationships, personal health, and the health of others through social awareness and effective communication.
Description: Predict how someone else may feel in a variety of situations and display compassionate and empathetic behaviors. [HE; SE]

Topic: Social Awareness, Relationship, and Communication Skills: Enhance relationships, personal health, and the health of others through social awareness and effective communication.
Description: Identify reasons why it is important to have positive social relationships (e.g., positive emotions, support and help, someone to play with). [HPE; SE]

Topic: Social Awareness, Relationship, and Communication Skills: Enhance relationships, personal health, and the health of others through social awareness and effective communication.
Description: Identify and practice strategies to make and keep friends, and develop positive peer relationships (e.g., identify and acknowledge other people?s feelings, communicate effectively, ask for help). [HPE; SE]

Topic: Social Awareness, Relationship, and Communication Skills: Enhance relationships, personal health, and the health of others through social awareness and effective communication.
Description: Identify and practice talking to trusted adults, parents/guardians, and/or family members about feelings. [HPE; SE]˙

Topic: Social Awareness, Relationship, and Communication Skills: Enhance relationships, personal health, and the health of others through social awareness and effective communication.
Description: Effectively express needs, wants, and feelings through both verbal and non-verbal actions. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Social Awareness, Relationship, and Communication Skills: Enhance relationships, personal health, and the health of others through social awareness and effective communication.
Description: Show respect for the feelings, rights, and property of others. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Social Awareness, Relationship, and Communication Skills: Enhance relationships, personal health, and the health of others through social awareness and effective communication.
Description: Demonstrate effective listening and communication skills, including giving and accepting a compliment and feedback, individually and in group settings. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Social Awareness, Relationship, and Communication Skills: Enhance relationships, personal health, and the health of others through social awareness and effective communication.
Description: Recognize and appreciate individual differences in others. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Social Awareness, Relationship, and Communication Skills: Enhance relationships, personal health, and the health of others through social awareness and effective communication.
Description: Describe positive qualities in self and others. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Social Awareness, Relationship, and Communication Skills: Enhance relationships, personal health, and the health of others through social awareness and effective communication.
Description: Identify reasons conflict and disagreements may arise in various situations and strategies for resolving misunderstandings and managing conflict. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Movement Skills: Demonstrate competence in, and knowledge of, a variety of movement forms, motor skills, physical fitness components, and principles in order to engage in purposeful and health-promoting physical activity, including sports and games.
Description: Demonstrate developmentally-appropriate locomotor movements (i.e., hop, gallop, jog, slide, skip, roll, and run) and movement concepts (i.e., traveling in various pathways, showing differentiation of speed) in dynamic environments (e.g., incorporated in a dance or rhythm activity, applied during a game or activity). [PE]

Topic: Movement Skills: Demonstrate competence in, and knowledge of, a variety of movement forms, motor skills, physical fitness components, and principles in order to engage in purposeful and health-promoting physical activity, including sports and games.
Description: Demonstrate different levels (low, middle and high), a variety of relationships with objects (i.e., over, under, around, through), and ability to vary speed and force while traveling.˙[PE]

Topic: Movement Skills: Demonstrate competence in, and knowledge of, a variety of movement forms, motor skills, physical fitness components, and principles in order to engage in purposeful and health-promoting physical activity, including sports and games.
Description: Demonstrate the ability to engage in rhythmic movement activities (e.g., move in self-space and general space in response to designated beats or rhythms, drumball, dance). [PE]

Topic: Movement Skills: Demonstrate competence in, and knowledge of, a variety of movement forms, motor skills, physical fitness components, and principles in order to engage in purposeful and health-promoting physical activity, including sports and games.
Description: Demonstrate developmentally-appropriate jumping and landing (i.e., using a variety of one- and two-foot take-offs and landings, and jump roping skills). [PE]

Topic: Movement Skills: Demonstrate competence in, and knowledge of, a variety of movement forms, motor skills, physical fitness components, and principles in order to engage in purposeful and health-promoting physical activity, including sports and games.
Description: Demonstrate stability skills by maintaining balance and transferring weight (one body part to another) on different bases of support and combining levels and shapes. [PE]

Topic: Movement Skills: Demonstrate competence in, and knowledge of, a variety of movement forms, motor skills, physical fitness components, and principles in order to engage in purposeful and health-promoting physical activity, including sports and games.
Description: Demonstrate developmentally-appropriate stationary body actions in non-dynamic environments.˙

Topic: Movement Skills: Demonstrate competence in, and knowledge of, a variety of movement forms, motor skills, physical fitness components, and principles in order to engage in purposeful and health-promoting physical activity, including sports and games.
Description: Demonstrate developmentally-appropriate manipulative skills in varying environments (i.e., underhand and overhand throwing in a non-dynamic environment, catching, dribbling with hands and feet, bouncing a ball, volleying, striking, rolling a ball in non-dynamic or dynamic environments, and kicking). [PE]

Topic: Self-awareness and Analyzing Influences: Examine how emotions, thoughts, needs, values, beliefs, and other factors (both internal and external) influence behaviors and articulate how these influences impact health behavior and outcomes.
Description: Recognize and accurately label simple emotions (e.g., happy, sad, mad, worried, lonely). [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-awareness and Analyzing Influences: Examine how emotions, thoughts, needs, values, beliefs, and other factors (both internal and external) influence behaviors and articulate how these influences impact health behavior and outcomes.
Description: Explain that emotions are information and that personal emotions may be the same or different from the emotions of others. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-awareness and Analyzing Influences: Examine how emotions, thoughts, needs, values, beliefs, and other factors (both internal and external) influence behaviors and articulate how these influences impact health behavior and outcomes.
Description: Demonstrate emotional regulation strategies to support mental and emotional health alone or with support from adults. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-awareness and Analyzing Influences: Examine how emotions, thoughts, needs, values, beliefs, and other factors (both internal and external) influence behaviors and articulate how these influences impact health behavior and outcomes.
Description: Accept failure and demonstrate the ability to persevere despite real or perceived failures. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-awareness and Analyzing Influences: Examine how emotions, thoughts, needs, values, beliefs, and other factors (both internal and external) influence behaviors and articulate how these influences impact health behavior and outcomes.
Description: Demonstrate growth-oriented practices by attempting, repeating, and experimenting with a variety of experiences and activities. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-awareness and Analyzing Influences: Examine how emotions, thoughts, needs, values, beliefs, and other factors (both internal and external) influence behaviors and articulate how these influences impact health behavior and outcomes.
Description: Describe personal strengths and the ways that those strengths support mental health. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-awareness and Analyzing Influences: Examine how emotions, thoughts, needs, values, beliefs, and other factors (both internal and external) influence behaviors and articulate how these influences impact health behavior and outcomes.
Description: Articulate and celebrate the individual characteristics that make a person unique, and explain that how a person views themselves can be influenced by different factors (e.g., peers, media, culture, family, phase of life). [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-awareness and Analyzing Influences: Examine how emotions, thoughts, needs, values, beliefs, and other factors (both internal and external) influence behaviors and articulate how these influences impact health behavior and outcomes.
Description: Demonstrate strategies that help all students feel welcome and valued as a part of the school community (e.g., cooperative playing, listening, showing you care, sharing). [HPE; SE]

Topic: Information and Resource Seeking: Access, evaluate, and use valid and reliable health information, products, services, and related resources.
Description: Identify school and community health helpers and community resources. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Information and Resource Seeking: Access, evaluate, and use valid and reliable health information, products, services, and related resources.
Description: Identify individuals who can assist with health-related issues and potentially life-threatening health conditions (e.g., asthma episodes, allergic reactions, seizures, concussions). [HE]

Topic: Information and Resource Seeking: Access, evaluate, and use valid and reliable health information, products, services, and related resources.
Description: Explain why it is important to use prescription and over-the-counter medicines correctly and safely, and the risks of ingesting household poisons (e.g., bleach, laundry detergent) and incorrectly using household products. [HE]

Topic: Information and Resource Seeking: Access, evaluate, and use valid and reliable health information, products, services, and related resources.
Description: Identify trusted adults at home and school (e.g., school nurse, guidance counselor) who can discuss rules and practices related to medicine use (e.g., only taking medicine with an adult?s help, only taking prescriptions that are prescribed to you). [HE]

Topic: Self-Advocacy and Health Promotion: Promote personal, family, and community health and well-being
Description: Demonstrate personal hygiene habits and other behaviors that prevent the spread of illness (including foodborne illness) and infection. [HPE]

Topic: Self-Advocacy and Health Promotion: Promote personal, family, and community health and well-being
Description: Describe personal health habits (i.e., brushing and flossing teeth, hygiene, hand washing, sufficient sleep, sun safety, physical activity, limiting screen time) that can prevent illness and promote self-care and overall health. [HPE]

Topic: Self-Advocacy and Health Promotion: Promote personal, family, and community health and well-being
Description: Self-advocate in order to have personal health needs met (e.g., needing to wash hands, asking for sun protection, access to bathrooms). [HPE]

Topic: Self-Advocacy and Health Promotion: Promote personal, family, and community health and well-being
Description: Recognize how the actions of others can impact physical health (e.g., spreading germs) and encourage peers to make positive choices about physical health habits and prevention strategies. [HPE]

Topic: Self-Advocacy and Health Promotion: Promote personal, family, and community health and well-being
Description: Identify the impact of personal activities that contribute, positively or negatively, to the environment. [HE]

Topic: Self-Advocacy and Health Promotion: Promote personal, family, and community health and well-being
Description: Identify ways that the communities people live in can impact their health and well-being. [HPE]

Topic: Self-Advocacy and Health Promotion: Promote personal, family, and community health and well-being
Description: Identify strategies to minimize impact on the environment (e.g., reduce, reuse, recycle). [HE]

Topic: Self-Advocacy and Health Promotion: Promote personal, family, and community health and well-being
Description: Encourage peers and family members to make choices to help protect the environment (e.g., recycling, using less water, turning off the lights). [HE]

Topic: Self-Advocacy and Health Promotion: Promote personal, family, and community health and well-being
Description: Encourage peers and family to help in the community (e.g., donate food to a food pantry, clean up litter). [HE]

Topic: Decision-making and Problem-Solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Identify principles of balanced eating to meet nutritional needs (e.g., moderation, eating a variety of fruits and vegetables, consuming nutrient-rich foods, limiting processed foods and foods high in added sugar, drinking water, limiting sugary beverages) when making nutrition-related decisions. [HPE]

Topic: Decision-making and Problem-Solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Identify and describe hunger and satiety cues and how these can inform nutrition-related decision-making. [HE]

Topic: Decision-making and Problem-Solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Discuss the benefits of balanced eating on physical health (e.g., supporting growth and development, ability to engage in physical activity), social health, and emotional and mental health (e.g., ability to manage stress, positive emotions) when making nutrition-related decisions. [HPE]

Topic: Decision-making and Problem-Solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Demonstrate how to use food labels as part of nutrition-related decision-making. [HE]

Topic: Decision-making and Problem-Solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Describe how cultivation, trade routes, and regions affect food supply, and how food production affects nutrition-related decisions. [HE]

Topic: Decision-making and Problem-Solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Identify circumstances (e.g., budget, food access and availability, time management) that help or hinder decisions about nutrition and determine when assistance is needed in order to make a health-promoting decision. [HPE]

Topic: Decision-making and Problem-Solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Explain a variety of factors that can influence decisions about nutrition (e.g., food during celebrations, food preferences, media advertising, celebrity endorsements and product placement, access and availability, financial resources, physical activity levels). [HPE]

Topic: Decision-making and Problem-Solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Recognize that individuals have different food related needs, preferences, and traditions. [HE]

Topic: Decision-making and Problem-Solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: List options, predict potential outcomes of decision options, make a health-promoting choice, and analyze the outcome of decisions in common nutrition situations. [HE]

Topic: Decision-making and Problem-Solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Analyze movement situations and apply the appropriate spatial, movement, and strategic concepts (e.g., force, direction, speed, pathways, extensions) in small-sided practice tasks, game environments, dance, and gymnastics. [PE]

Topic: Decision-making and Problem-Solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Apply basic offensive and defensive strategies and tactics in a variety of activities and small-sided games. [PE]

Topic: Decision-making and Problem-Solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Apply movement concepts, principles and strategies to avoid objects within dynamic environments [PE]

Topic: Decision-making and Problem-Solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Analyze and modify a movement based on the demands of the task and/or environmental constraints in dynamic and changing situations. [PE]

Topic: Decision-making and Problem-Solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Adjust performance of locomotor, manipulative, and stability skills based on environmental and task constraints and ensure safe physical activity participation of the individual and peers. [PE]

Topic: Decision-making and Problem-Solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Demonstrate effective decision-making skills while engaged in emerging and maturing skill performance settings (including dance and gymnastics), games, and activities. [PE]

Topic: Decision-making and Problem-Solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Engage in independent and cooperative problem-solving activities while participating in physical activities. [PE; SE]

Topic: Decision-making and Problem-Solving: Make health-promoting, informed, responsible decisions and solve problems in a variety of health-related situations.
Description: Compare benefits of various physical activities in order to support personal decision-making related to physical activity. [PE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors.
Description: Identify different feelings and emotions (e.g., anger, fear, grief, sadness, anxiety, stress, hopelessness, gratitude, love, excitement, contentedness, hope, pride, happiness) that people may experience and how people might express those emotions (including individual and cultural differences in expression). [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors.
Description: Discuss how feelings and emotions can impact behavior. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors.
Description: Recognize that all feelings and emotions are information that individuals can use to support mental and emotional health. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors.
Description: Identify how a person?s brain and body influence mental and emotional well-being. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors.
Description: Describe and demonstrate strategies for expressing and regulating emotions in health-promoting ways. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors.
Description: Identify characteristics of and practices to support mental and emotional well-being within various cultures and diverse perspectives. [HE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors.
Description: Set a goal to use one or more health-promoting practices or behaviors (e.g., being aware of your own feelings and the feelings of others, safe online behaviors, engaging in physical activity, limiting screen time) and track progress towards its achievement in order to maintain or improve mental and emotional well-being. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors.
Description: Apply self-monitoring strategies, alone or with support, to regulate emotions (e.g., breathing techniques, appropriate sleep, proper nutrition) in varied settings. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors.
Description: Identify personal stressors and demonstrate effective stress management techniques, alone or with support. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors.
Description: Demonstrate strategies and behaviors (which may include getting help) to help meet personal responsibilities and identify strategies to overcome barriers to meeting personal responsibilities. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors.
Description: Identify strategies for planning, prioritizing, and managing time. [HE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors.
Description: Demonstrate strategies that support a growth mindset in and out of school. [HPE; SE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors.
Description: Describe the physical, social, and emotional changes that occur during puberty and adolescence, how the timing of puberty and adolescent development varies considerably, and the role of puberty in overall development. [HE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors.
Description: Recognize and respect that all bodies are different. [HPE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors.
Description: Explain a variety of health-promoting practices to manage the social, physical, and emotional changes associated with puberty and into adolescence. [HPE]

Topic: Self-management and Goal Setting: Set goals, engage in health-promoting behaviors, and avoid risky behaviors.
Description: Describe health-promoting behaviors during menstruation, including ways to maintain personal hygiene, cope with emotional changes, manage pain and identify when help or support is needed. [HE]
